title: How to Create Branded Short Links: A Complete Guide for 2024
excerpt: Step-by-step guide to creating professional branded short links with custom domains. Learn how to set up, customize, and optimize your branded URLs for maximum impact.
content:
What Are Branded Short Links?

Branded short links use your own custom domain instead of generic shorteners. For example, go.yourbrand.com/offer instead of bit.ly/xyz123.

Benefits of Branded Links

  • Trust: Users are 34% more likely to click branded links
  • Recognition: Every link reinforces your brand
  • Control: You own the domain and data
  • SEO: Branded domains can improve search rankings
  • Professional: Looks better in all communications

Step 1: Choose Your Domain

Select a short, memorable domain or subdomain:

  • go.yourbrand.com
  • link.yourbrand.com
  • get.yourbrand.com
  • try.yourbrand.com

Step 2: Set Up with Keynou Drive

  1. Sign up for Keynou Drive
  2. Navigate to Settings > Custom Domains
  3. Add your chosen domain/subdomain
  4. Copy the CNAME record provided

Step 3: Configure DNS

Add a CNAME record in your DNS settings:

  • Type: CNAME
  • Name: go (or your chosen subdomain)
  • Value: [provided by Keynou Drive]
  • TTL: 3600

Step 4: Verify Domain

Return to Keynou Drive and click "Verify Domain". DNS propagation usually takes 5-30 minutes.

Step 5: Create Your First Branded Link

  1. Click "Create Link"
  2. Enter destination URL
  3. Choose custom slug (e.g., spring-sale)
  4. Add tracking parameters
  5. Customize splash screen (optional)
  6. Click "Create"

Best Practices

Slug Naming

  • Keep it short and memorable
  • Use keywords when relevant
  • Avoid special characters
  • Make it descriptive

Consistency

  • Use the same domain across all campaigns
  • Maintain a naming convention
  • Document your link strategy

Tracking

  • Always add UTM parameters
  • Monitor click-through rates
  • A/B test different slugs
  • Review analytics regularly

Advanced Features

QR Codes

Keynou Drive automatically generates QR codes for all branded links. Perfect for print materials and events.

Link Expiration

Set expiration dates for time-sensitive campaigns. Links automatically redirect to a fallback URL after expiration.

Geo-Targeting

Redirect users to different destinations based on their location. Great for international campaigns.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Using too many different domains
  • Creating overly complex slugs
  • Not tracking link performance
  • Forgetting to test links before sharing
  • Using generic slugs like "link1" or "test"

Get Started Today

Ready to create professional branded short links? Sign up for Keynou Drive and set up your custom domain in minutes.
author: Emily Watson
metaTitle: How to Create Branded Short Links: Complete 2024 Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to create professional branded short links with custom domains. Step-by-step guide covering setup, DNS configuration, best practices, and optimization tips.

title: UTM Parameters Guide: How to Track Your Marketing Campaigns Effectively
excerpt: Master UTM parameters to track campaign performance in Google Analytics. Learn best practices for naming conventions, parameter structure, and campaign attribution.
content:
What Are UTM Parameters?

UTM (Urchin Tracking Module) parameters are tags added to URLs to track the effectiveness of marketing campaigns in Google Analytics.

The Five UTM Parameters

1. utm_source (Required)

Identifies the source of your traffic:

  • utm_source=facebook
  • utm_source=newsletter
  • utm_source=google

2. utm_medium (Required)

Identifies the marketing medium:

  • utm_medium=social
  • utm_medium=email
  • utm_medium=cpc

3. utm_campaign (Required)

Identifies the specific campaign:

  • utm_campaign=spring_sale
  • utm_campaign=product_launch
  • utm_campaign=webinar_2024

4. utm_term (Optional)

Identifies paid search keywords:

  • utm_term=url+shortener
  • utm_term=branded+links

5. utm_content (Optional)

Differentiates similar content or links:

  • utm_content=header_cta
  • utm_content=footer_link
  • utm_content=button_blue

Example URL with UTM Parameters

https://drive.keynou.com/signup?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=spring_sale&utm_content=video_ad

Best Practices

Naming Conventions

  • Use lowercase for consistency
  • Use underscores instead of spaces
  • Be descriptive but concise
  • Maintain a naming document

Common Values

Sources: facebook, twitter, linkedin, newsletter, blog, google

Mediums: social, email, cpc, organic, referral, display

Campaigns: Use descriptive names that identify the promotion

Using UTM Parameters with Keynou Drive

Keynou Drive makes UTM tracking effortless:

  1. Create a short link
  2. Add UTM parameters in the dashboard
  3. Keynou Drive automatically appends them
  4. Track performance in real-time
  5. View analytics in Google Analytics

Campaign Tracking Strategy

Email Campaigns

utm_source=newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=weekly_digest

Social Media

utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=thought_leadership

Paid Advertising

utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=brand_keywords&utm_term=url+shortener

Common Mistakes

  • Inconsistent naming (Facebook vs facebook)
  • Using spaces instead of underscores
  • Not documenting your conventions
  • Forgetting to add UTM parameters
  • Using too many variations

Analyzing UTM Data

In Google Analytics, navigate to:

  • Acquisition > Campaigns > All Campaigns
  • View source/medium reports
  • Compare campaign performance
  • Track conversions by campaign

Start Tracking Today

Sign up for Keynou Drive and start tracking your campaigns with automated UTM parameter management.
author: David Kim
metaTitle: UTM Parameters Guide: Track Marketing Campaigns Like a Pro
metaDescription: Complete guide to UTM parameters for campaign tracking. Learn best practices, naming conventions, and how to use UTM tags with Keynou Drive for better analytics.

title: QR Codes + Short Links: The Ultimate Offline-to-Online Marketing Strategy
excerpt: Combine QR codes with branded short links to bridge offline and online marketing. Learn how to create, track, and optimize QR code campaigns with Keynou Drive.
content:
The Power of QR Codes in 2024

QR codes have made a massive comeback. With smartphone cameras now reading QR codes natively, they've become an essential tool for connecting offline experiences to online content.

Why Combine QR Codes with Short Links?

1. Trackability

When you generate a QR code from a short link, you can track:

  • Total scans
  • Location data
  • Time of scan
  • Device information
  • Conversion rates

2. Flexibility

Update the destination URL without reprinting QR codes. Your printed materials stay relevant longer.

3. Branding

Use branded short links in your QR codes to maintain brand consistency even in the URL.

4. Professional Appearance

A QR code linking to go.yourbrand.com/menu looks more professional than a long, complex URL.

Creating QR Codes with Keynou Drive

  1. Create a branded short link
  2. Click "Generate QR Code"
  3. Customize design (colors, logo)
  4. Download in high resolution
  5. Add to your marketing materials

Use Cases for QR Code + Short Link Campaigns

Restaurant Menus

Replace physical menus with QR codes linking to digital menus. Update prices and items without reprinting.

Product Packaging

Add QR codes to packaging for:

  • Product information
  • Assembly instructions
  • Warranty registration
  • Customer reviews

Event Marketing

Use QR codes on:

  • Posters and flyers
  • Name badges
  • Presentation slides
  • Booth displays

Retail Stores

Place QR codes for:

  • Product details
  • Customer reviews
  • Special offers
  • Loyalty programs

Real Estate

Add QR codes to:

  • For Sale signs
  • Property brochures
  • Open house flyers
  • Business cards

QR Code Design Best Practices

Size and Placement

  • Minimum size: 2cm x 2cm
  • Add white space around code
  • Place at eye level when possible
  • Ensure good lighting

Testing

  • Test with multiple devices
  • Test from various distances
  • Verify link destination
  • Check loading speed

Call to Action

Always include text near your QR code:

  • "Scan to view menu"
  • "Scan for special offer"
  • "Scan to learn more"
  • "Scan to register"

Tracking QR Code Performance

With Keynou Drive, you can:

  • See total scans in real-time
  • Compare performance across campaigns
  • Identify peak scanning times
  • Analyze geographic distribution
  • Calculate ROI on print materials

Advanced QR Code Strategies

Dynamic QR Codes

Use short links to create dynamic QR codes that you can update without reprinting.

A/B Testing

Create multiple QR codes with different designs or placements to test performance.

Geo-Targeting

Redirect users to different content based on their location when they scan.

Time-Based Campaigns

Change the destination URL based on time of day or date.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Making QR codes too small
  • Using low-contrast colors
  • Not testing before printing
  • Forgetting to add a call-to-action
  • Linking to non-mobile-friendly pages
  • Not tracking performance

Get Started with QR Codes

Sign up for Keynou Drive and start creating trackable QR codes with branded short links today.
author: Jessica Martinez
metaTitle: QR Codes + Short Links: Complete Offline Marketing Guide 2024
metaDescription: Master QR code marketing with branded short links. Learn to create, track, and optimize QR campaigns for restaurants, retail, events, and more with Keynou Drive.
